title: Chern classes of Laughlin bundles on the quasihole moduli space
abstract: We study fractional quantum Hall states with quasihole excitations, on Riemann surfaces of arbitrary genus. For configurations with $m$ quasiholes we construct a vector bundle above the $m$-th symmetric power of the curve so that the fiber at a point $\lbrace w_1,\dots,w_m \rbrace$ corresponds to the state with quasiholes localized at these positions. We determine the Chern character of this bundle via the Grothendieck-Riemann-Roch theorem and show that in the completely filled state, i.e. when the number of particles is maximal, the vector bundle is compatible with the condition of projective flatness. Furthermore, we obtain a generalization of this result to the case of multiple layers and multiple quasihole types. In genus zero and one, we construct explicit wave-functions and verify that the curvature of the associated Chern connection reproduces the predicted Chern classes. The Chern classes obtained match, term by term, the predicted decomposition of the Berry phase under quasihole exchange, into an extensive Aharonov--Bohm contribution and a fractional statistical contribution.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous topological description of Laughlin quasihole states on arbitrary Riemann surfaces. By computing the Chern character of the quasihole bundle, the authors mathematically link the topological invariants of the bundle to the physical fractional statistics and Aharonov-Bohm phases observed in the fractional quantum Hall effect.
main_problem: The paper aims to compute the Chern character and Chern classes of the Laughlin quasihole bundle over the quasihole moduli space on Riemann surfaces of arbitrary genus.
main_result: The authors derive a general formula for the Chern character of the quasihole bundle and show that in the completely filled state, the bundle is compatible with projective flatness, with results matching the predicted Berry phase decomposition.
method: The study employs the Grothendieck-Riemann-Roch theorem, the Abel-Jacobi map, and the Chern-Weil theory, alongside explicit verification in genus 0 and 1 using the Chern connection and theta functions.
model_or_system: The system consists of single-layer and multi-layer Laughlin fractional quantum Hall states on Riemann surfaces of genus g, featuring particles and quasiholes with specific vanishing orders (b and c) and interaction matrices (K and C).
key_observables: Chern character, Chern classes, Berry phase (Aharonov-Bohm and fractional statistical contributions), and Hall conductance.
important_parameters: Genus (g), number of particles (n), number of quasiholes (m), particle-particle vanishing order (b), particle-quasihole vanishing order (c), and magnetic line bundle degree (d).
main_assumptions: The calculation assumes the wave-functions can be analytically continued and that the chosen frame of sections is holomorphic in the quasihole positions.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the geometric construction of the quasihole bundle, proceeds to derive the Chern character using the Grothendieck-Riemann-Roch theorem, provides explicit low-genus verifications, and concludes with a generalization to multi-layer systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
